headline: Lululemon Names Former Nike Executive as Its CEO. The Stock Is Down.
summary: Lululemon  Athletica has a new CEO, but investors seem skeptical she can fix the athletic wear maker’s old problems.  After the close of trading Wednesday, Lululemon said Heidi O’Neill would be its new chief executive, effective Sept. 8.  O’Neill certainly has a lot of work ahead of her: Lululemon stock is down more than 50% in the past five years, and has lost more than 20% in 2026 alone.

headline: Market reacts coolly as Lululemon taps ex-Nike exec Heidi O’Neill for top job
summary: Investing.com -- Lululemon Athletica (NASDAQ:LULU) shares declined 5% in after-hours trading Wednesday following the company’s announcement that it has appointed Heidi O’Neill as its next Chief Executive Officer.

headline: Lululemon taps former Nike executive to be next CEO

headline: Lululemon Names Nike Veteran Heidi O’Neill as Next CEO
summary: O’Neill said she plans to "accelerate product breakthroughs, deepen the brand’s cultural relevance" when she joins in September.

headline: lululemon Names Proven Brand Builder Heidi O’Neill as Chief Executive Officer
summary: VANCOUVER, British Columbia, April 22, 2026--lululemon athletica inc. (NASDAQ:LULU) today announced that, following a comprehensive search process, the company’s Board of Directors has unanimously approved the appointment of industry veteran Heidi O’Neill as the company’s next Chief Executive Officer. Ms. O’Neill will start as CEO and join the Board effective September 8, 2026, and will be based in Vancouver.

raw.headline: lululemon Names Proven Brand Builder Heidi O’Neill as Chief Executive Officer
raw.summary: VANCOUVER, British Columbia, April 22, 2026--lululemon athletica inc. (NASDAQ:LULU) today announced that, following a comprehensive search process, the company’s Board of Directors has unanimously approved the appointment of industry veteran Heidi O’Neill as the company’s next Chief Executive Officer. Ms. O’Neill will start as CEO and join the Board effective September 8, 2026, and will be based in Vancouver.
